entry.text: Sun on cobbles. The well sits quiet. A flour sack rests by the crates.

use.say: You step back into the tavern.

look.say: A stone well. Cool water, but the King asked for ale, not a drink from the yard.

use.say: You splash a little water on your hands. Refreshing — not dinner.

look.say: Just crates now. You took the flour.
A tied sack of fine flour. Perfect for a golden loaf.

take.say: You hoist the flour sack. The oven upstairs will do the rest.

use.say: You hoist the flour sack. The oven upstairs will do the rest.

look.say: Storage crates. A flour sack sits with them.

use.say: If you need flour, take the sack.

take.say: The crates are empty of everything but air — except that flour sack.

look.say: The gate to the road. Guests will come through here — after you set the feast.

use.say: No time for a stroll. The King dines soon.

objectives.text: Goal: bake the flour in the hearth oven
Goal: take the flour sack by the crates
